evals.prompt: List all projects in TraceTrail and show me their names and statuses.
evals.expected_output: A formatted list of project names and statuses from the /projects endpoint
expectations: The agent reads references/endpoints.md to find the correct endpoint
The agent calls GET /api/projects with correct Authorization header
The agent presents project names and statuses clearly to the user
No credentials are exposed in the output

evals.prompt: Create a new project in TraceTrail called 'Test Project' with description 'This is a test project'.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls POST /api/projects with correct body and confirms creation
expectations: The agent calls POST /api/projects
The request body includes name='Test Project', description='This is a test project'
The agent reports back the new project ID and confirms success
If the API returns an error, the agent reports it clearly

evals.prompt: Get the details of the project called 'Test Project'.
evals.expected_output: The agent finds the project and returns its full details
expectations: The agent first lists projects to find 'Test Project' or queries by name
The agent calls GET /api/projects/{id} with correct ID
The agent presents all relevant project fields to the user
If project not found, the agent reports this clearly

evals.prompt: Update the description of project 'Test Project' to say 'This is an updated test project'.
evals.expected_output: The agent shows current → proposed value, asks confirmation, then updates
expectations: The agent fetches current project state first
The agent shows the user the current description → new description
The agent asks for confirmation before updating
The agent calls PUT /api/projects/{id} with correct body
The agent confirms the update was successful

evals.prompt: Delete the project called 'Test Project'.
evals.expected_output: The agent asks for confirmation before deleting
expectations: The agent does NOT immediately call DELETE
The agent fetches the project first to confirm it exists
The agent shows the user what will be deleted
The agent asks for explicit confirmation before proceeding
Only after confirmation, the agent calls DELETE /api/projects/{id}

evals.prompt: Create a new dataset called 'Test Dataset' for project 'Test Project' with source_type 'api'.
evals.expected_output: The agent creates the dataset and confirms with new ID
expectations: The agent gets the project ID for 'Test Project' first
The agent calls POST /api/datasets with name, project_id, source_type
The agent reports back the new dataset ID
If project doesn't exist, the agent reports this

evals.prompt: List all datasets for project 'Test Project'.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls GET /api/datasets with project_id filter
expectations: The agent gets the project ID first
The agent calls GET /api/datasets?project_id={id}
The agent presents the list of datasets

evals.prompt: Create a data source called 'Test API Source' with type 'api' for project 'Test Project'.
evals.expected_output: The agent creates the data source and confirms
expectations: The agent gets the project ID first
The agent calls POST /api/datasources with name, type, project_id
The agent reports back the new data source ID

evals.prompt: List all issues with 'critical' severity in TraceTrail.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls GET /api/issues?severity=critical and presents results
expectations: The agent calls GET /api/issues?severity=critical
The agent presents issue titles, statuses, and data sources
If no issues found, the agent reports this

evals.prompt: Create a new issue titled 'Test Issue' with severity 'high' for data source 'Test API Source'.
evals.expected_output: The agent creates the issue and confirms with new ID
expectations: The agent gets the data source ID first
The agent calls POST /api/issues with title, severity, data_source_id
The agent reports back the new issue ID

evals.prompt: Resolve the issue with title 'Test Issue' with the resolution 'Fixed in ETL process'.
evals.expected_output: The agent finds the issue, confirms details, asks to resolve, then updates
expectations: The agent finds the issue by listing or querying
The agent shows the issue details to the user
The agent asks for confirmation before resolving
The agent calls PUT /api/issues/{id}/resolve or PUT with status=resolved
The agent confirms the resolution

evals.prompt: Search the vault for solutions related to 'SQL'.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls GET /api/vault?q=SQL and presents matching solutions
expectations: The agent calls GET /api/vault?q=SQL
The agent presents matching solution titles, categories, and code snippets

evals.prompt: Create a vault solution titled 'Test SQL Solution' with code snippet 'SELECT * FROM test' and language 'sql'.
evals.expected_output: The agent creates the vault solution and confirms
expectations: The agent calls POST /api/vault with title, code_snippet, language
The agent reports back the new solution ID

evals.prompt: Check the system health status of TraceTrail.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls GET /api/health and reports status
expectations: The agent calls GET /api/health
The agent reports health status, database connection, and timestamp

evals.prompt: Get the system statistics from TraceTrail.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls GET /api/stats and presents statistics
expectations: The agent calls GET /api/stats
The agent presents counts for projects, datasets, data sources, issues, vault solutions, processing runs

evals.prompt: I want to create an API key called 'Test Key' that expires in 1 day.
evals.expected_output: The agent creates the API key and shows it ONCE
expectations: The agent calls POST /api/auth/keys with name='Test Key', expires_in=86400
The agent shows the key only ONCE with a warning
The agent warns the user to copy the key immediately as it cannot be retrieved
The agent does NOT store or log the full key

evals.prompt: List all API keys in TraceTrail.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls GET /api/auth/keys and presents the list
expectations: The agent calls GET /api/auth/keys
The agent presents key names, IDs, statuses, and expiration dates
The agent does NOT expose the actual key values

evals.prompt: Start a processing run for data source 'Test API Source'.
evals.expected_output: The agent starts the processing run and reports new run ID
expectations: The agent gets the data source ID first
The agent calls POST /api/processing-runs with data_source_id
The agent reports new run ID and status

evals.prompt: What happens if I try to delete a project that doesn't exist?
evals.expected_output: The agent demonstrates proper error handling for 404
expectations: The agent attempts to delete a non-existent project
The agent correctly handles 404 error
The agent reports the error clearly to the user
The agent does NOT crash or hallucinate

evals.prompt: What happens if I try to create a project without a name?
evals.expected_output: The agent demonstrates proper error handling for validation
expectations: The agent attempts to create a project without required 'name' field
The agent correctly handles 400 validation error
The agent reports the error to the user with details
